title: Victoria Vivian Chan

title: Victoria Vivian Chan
text:
Quick Overview
I am currently completing a B.S. in Statistics & Data Science at UCLA and will begin an M.S. in Quantitative Finance at the National University of Singapore in 2026.
My experience includes Data Science and Corporate Development internships at GoTo, leadership in the Indonesian Bruin Student Association, and project work in statistical learning, experimental design, and public-facing data storytelling.

title: Victoria Vivian Chan
text:
Featured Highlights

GoTo Data Science Intern — credit risk model monitoring, drift analysis, and model performance evaluation

Beyond the Board — public-facing education inequality website

Skin Cancer Classification Project — compared GLM, LDA, and Random Forest models

Music and Running Study — factorial ANOVA on music, exercise intensity, running time, and heart rate

title: About
text:
I’m a Statistics & Data Science student at UCLA with a strong interest in quantitative analysis, finance, and data-driven storytelling. My academic and project work has helped me build a foundation in statistical modeling, inference, machine learning, and experimental design, while also showing me the importance of clear communication and context.
What draws me most to this field is the ability to turn complex information into insight. I enjoy the full process of analytical work: asking the right questions, cleaning and understanding the data, selecting methods carefully, and presenting results in a way that is useful and persuasive. For me, strong analysis is not only about technical correctness, but also about clarity, structure, and impact.
Beyond coursework, I’ve explored this through internships, research-based projects, and leadership experiences. My work has included credit risk and data science at GoTo, public-facing digital humanities research, and student-led projects in statistical learning and applied inference. These experiences have strengthened both my technical skills and my interest in solving real-world problems with data.
I’m especially interested in opportunities at the intersection of analytics, finance, and decision-making. Looking ahead, I hope to continue building work that combines quantitative rigor with strong communication, whether in risk, strategy, data science, or broader analytical roles.

title: About
text:
Interests

Data science

Applying statistical methods and programming to analyze complex datasets and uncover meaningful patterns.

Quantitative finance

Using mathematical and statistical models to understand financial markets, risk, and investment strategies.

Statistical learning

Building and evaluating predictive models such as classification and regression methods to make data-driven predictions.

Data storytelling

Transforming complex analyses into clear visualizations and narratives that communicate insights effectively.

Research communication

Presenting statistical findings and technical results in ways that are accessible and meaningful to different audiences.

Food exploration

I enjoy discovering new restaurants and cuisines, and documenting food experiences through photography and social media.

UCLA

UCLA

title: Statistics, data storytelling, and quantitative thinking for real-world decisions.
text:
A little about me
I’m a Statistics & Data Science student at UCLA with interests in data science, quantitative finance, analytics, and research communication. I like building work that is both technically strong and easy for people to understand, whether that means a model, a report, or a public-facing website.
My experience includes Data Science and Corporate Development internships at GoTo, leadership work at UCLA, and projects across statistical learning, experimental design, and narrative-driven digital research.

title: Statistics, data storytelling, and quantitative thinking for real-world decisions.
text:
Welcome
I’m Victoria Vivian Chan, a UCLA Statistics & Data Science student with interests in data science, quantitative finance, analytics, and research communication. I enjoy building work that is both technically strong and easy to understand, whether that takes the form of a model, report, presentation, or public-facing website.
My experience includes internships in data science and corporate development at GoTo, leadership roles at UCLA, and projects across statistical learning, experimental design, and digital humanities. Across all of these, I care about using data thoughtfully and communicating ideas clearly.
